title: Melting Asphalt in {city.name}
trigger: Temperatures hit record highs and the morgues are running out of space.
description: The asphalt is literally melting near {city.landmark}. The {city.government_body} advises citizens to think cool thoughts as air conditioning units overload the grid.

options.label: Open public cooling centers and distribute free ice.
options.advisor_reason: We lose 150M, but we keep people alive and grateful. It is expensive, but dodging a mass casualty event does wonders for our approval ratings.

consequences.follow_up: Lives are saved but the budget melts faster than the ice. The cooling centers smell terrible.
consequences.city_reaction: Sitting in a gymnasium with 500 sweaty strangers to survive. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Mandate a city-wide siesta with all businesses closed during peak heat hours.
options.advisor_reason: Closing businesses saves 70M compared to the cooling centers. People will complain about lost wages, but at least they will not be dying in the streets.

consequences.follow_up: The economy tanks but heatstroke cases drop. The {city.primary_industry_workers} lose a week of wages.
consequences.city_reaction: Forced naps are cool until you cannot pay rent. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Spray the streets with liquid nitrogen to cool them down.
options.advisor_reason: The 300M price tag is a nightmare, but perhaps if we freeze the city the media will focus on our bold climate action instead of the shattered roads.

consequences.follow_up: The rapid cooling shatters the asphalt completely. Three stray dogs are accidentally frozen solid.
consequences.city_reaction: From an oven to a freezer burn. Great job. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Toxic Tide of {city.name}
trigger: A chemical plant leak turns the main river into a toxic bubbling sludge.
description: Dead fish are piling up on the banks near {city.landmark}. The {city.media_outlet} discovers the plant's environmental inspector is legally blind and also the mayor's cousin.

options.label: Fine the company heavily and hire a professional cleanup crew.
options.advisor_reason: It costs us 100M up front, but cleaning the river buys us some much-needed public goodwill. I am tired of watching fish dissolve near {city.landmark}.

consequences.follow_up: The fine barely covers the cleanup but the river stops bubbling. The company threatens to relocate jobs.
consequences.city_reaction: Fined them pocket change for poisoning our water. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Shut down the plant permanently.
options.advisor_reason: A 200M economic hit is brutal, but shutting them down permanently secures our legacy. I just hope the unemployed workers appreciate the clean water.

consequences.follow_up: The environment heals but thousands of {city.primary_industry_workers} lose their jobs instantly.
consequences.city_reaction: River is clean but my wallet is empty. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Rebrand the toxic river as a hot springs spa experience.
options.advisor_reason: We only spend 50M and pretend it is a public wellness initiative. It is a terrible lie, but I am too exhausted to fight the mayor's cousin on this.

consequences.follow_up: Tourists get chemical burns. The city is sued into oblivion by international health organizations.
consequences.city_reaction: My skin is peeling off but it is exfoliating. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Slipping Away in {city.name}
trigger: A severe storm washes away a chunk of the coastline leaving infrastructure teetering over the edge.
description: Wealthy property owners demand a taxpayer-funded seawall immediately, while inland residents demand the money be spent on basic drainage. The {city.government_body} is paralyzed by the competing lobbying.

options.label: Build the seawall to protect coastal property, angering the inlanders.
options.advisor_reason: Losing 300M is devastating, but letting the wealthy drown just causes worse headaches later. I hate ignoring the inlanders, but we need those property taxes.

consequences.follow_up: The coastline is saved. The inland districts flood during the next rain. The inequality is staggering.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad my taxes saved a hotel I cannot afford to look at. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Let the coastline erode and invest in inland drainage instead.
options.advisor_reason: We save half the budget and finally help the people who actually need drainage. The billionaires will scream, but ordinary citizens will love us for once.

consequences.follow_up: Coastal properties collapse spectacularly. Inland flooding stops. The city loses major coastal tax revenue.
consequences.city_reaction: Watching a billionaire's hotel sink was the best TV all year. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Try to physically push the eroding structures inland with bulldozers.
options.advisor_reason: It costs 120M and we at least look like we are taking aggressive urban planning action. When it collapses, we can just say we tried our best to save them.

consequences.follow_up: The structural integrity fails immediately. Everything crumbles into expensive rubble.
consequences.city_reaction: Did they think it was made of Lego. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Dust and Bones in {city.name}
trigger: The city's main reservoir drops to 20% capacity, revealing a sunken village and several rusted cars.
description: Water rationing is imminent. The {city.primary_industry_workers} demand priority access while golf courses continue watering their greens at midnight. The {city.media_outlet} publishes photos of the mayor's lush private lawn.

options.label: Implement strict residential water rationing and fine violators.
options.advisor_reason: It is deeply unpopular, but a 50M cost is manageable. I wish we could touch the golf courses, but rationing the poor is the only way we survive this.

consequences.follow_up: Citizens are showering once a week. The golf courses quietly pay the fines and keep watering anyway.
consequences.city_reaction: I smell terrible but the 18th hole looks great. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Cut off water to all luxury and recreational facilities.
options.advisor_reason: It costs 80M, but cutting off the elite will skyrocket our approval with the commoners. I have been wanting to dry out those private greens for years.

consequences.follow_up: The golf courses turn brown. The elites are furious and launch a smear campaign against the administration.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally the rich have to suffer with us. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Hire a rainmaker shaman for a massive public ceremony.
options.advisor_reason: Spending 150M on magic is absurd, but maybe giving the public a massive spectacle will distract them from the fact that we are completely out of water.

consequences.follow_up: The shaman dances for three days. It does not rain. The city is audited for the fraudulent expense.
consequences.city_reaction: We paid a guy to dance at the clouds. We are doomed. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Rivers of {city.name}
trigger: An hour of heavy rain overwhelms the clogged storm drains turning downtown into a river.
description: Commuters are paddling to work in inflatable kayaks. The {city.government_body} blames unforeseen weather despite it raining every year at this time. Trash from near {city.landmark} is floating past the banks.

options.label: Deploy emergency pumps and pay massive overtime for cleanup.
options.advisor_reason: Pumping it out costs 200M, but we have to save downtown. If we let the citizens drown, my office will never hear the end of the complaints.

consequences.follow_up: The water recedes, leaving a thick layer of mud and a massive bill for the city.
consequences.city_reaction: Smells like sewage but at least I can walk again. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Blame the citizens for clogging the drains with litter and refuse to pump.
options.advisor_reason: We only spend 30M if we just shift the blame onto them. It destroys our approval, but honestly, they do drop a lot of trash near {city.landmark}.

consequences.follow_up: The water stagnates for days breeding mosquitoes. The mayor's approval rating sinks faster than the cars.
consequences.city_reaction: Gaslighting us while we drown. Classic. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Stock the flooded streets with fish and declare it a new fishing district.
options.advisor_reason: Turning downtown into an urban fishery for 60M might be the only way to spin this disaster. Maybe the public will appreciate the new food source.

consequences.follow_up: People actually start fishing from office windows. The health department has a collective breakdown.
consequences.city_reaction: Caught a catfish in the bank lobby. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Yellow Fog of {city.name}
trigger: A thick yellow smog descends on the city reducing visibility to near zero.
description: Hospitals are flooded with asthma cases. The mayor is caught on camera boarding a private jet to a mountain resort for their health. The {city.media_outlet} is advising people to breathe through wet socks.

options.label: Distribute masks and mandate a temporary factory shutdown.
options.advisor_reason: The 150M hit is rough, but masks will keep the public breathing and our approval up. I would like to finally sleep without coughing up gray phlegm.

consequences.follow_up: The smog clears after a few days. The factory owners sue the city for lost revenue.
consequences.city_reaction: I can see the sun again. It is a miracle. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Ban all cars from the city center indefinitely.
options.advisor_reason: We lose 80M and people will hate the walk, but this is our one chance to pedestrianize the city center. The environment needs this win.

consequences.follow_up: The air clears but the {city.primary_industry_workers} riot because they cannot get to work. Commutes double.
consequences.city_reaction: Air is clean but I have to walk 10 miles to work. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Install giant industrial fans on {city.landmark} to blow the smog away.
options.advisor_reason: It costs 250M to just blow our problems onto the suburbs, but people love big visible infrastructure. At least it looks like we care about the air.

consequences.follow_up: The fans just blow the smog into the wealthy suburbs. The rich are furious. The poor find it hilarious.
consequences.city_reaction: Sharing the air quality crisis with the billionaires. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Ash Snow in {city.name}
trigger: Smoke from nearby unregulated agricultural fires chokes the entire city.
description: Ash is falling like snow. The {city.media_outlet} reports that several council members own the farms responsible for the fires. The {city.government_body} calls it a natural seasonal event.

options.label: Send city fire trucks to put out the agricultural fires.
options.advisor_reason: Spending 100M to fix the council's mess is infuriating, but the people will thank us for the clean air. We just have to hope downtown does not ignite.

consequences.follow_up: The fires are put out but the city is left defenseless when a real building catches fire downtown.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad the farms are safe while my apartment burns. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Publicly fine the council members who own the farms.
options.advisor_reason: It only costs 50M and the public will absolutely adore us for punishing the corrupt council. I am tired of covering for their profitable little fires.

consequences.follow_up: The council members retaliate by blocking the mayor's entire legislative agenda. Political gridlock ensues.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally holding them accountable even if nothing gets done now. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Tell citizens to collect the ash and sell it as premium artisan fertilizer.
options.advisor_reason: We barely spend 30M if we convince them it is a green economic opportunity. I know the ash is toxic, but the administration is forcing my hand here.

consequences.follow_up: The ash is toxic. Hospitals fill with people who touched it. The city is sued internationally.
consequences.city_reaction: The mayor's side hustle is poisoning us. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Mountain Moves in {city.name}
trigger: Days of rain cause a massive landslide in a densely populated hillside settlement.
description: Hundreds are displaced. The {city.government_body} had ignored warnings for years, claiming the settlement was technically outside city limits. The {city.media_outlet} is broadcasting the devastation around the clock.

options.label: Set up emergency shelters and formally accept responsibility.
options.advisor_reason: A 250M bailout is going to ruin our budget, but we have to take care of these displaced families. It is the only humane urban policy we have left.

consequences.follow_up: The city avoids a riot but the financial cost is staggering. The mayor's career is permanently stained.
consequences.city_reaction: A little late for apologies but we need the tents. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Blame the residents for building illegal structures and refuse all aid.
options.advisor_reason: We save 220M by washing our hands of it. It breaks my heart and our approval rating, but technically they should not have built there anyway.

consequences.follow_up: International human rights groups condemn the city. The displaced residents march on city hall.
consequences.city_reaction: Heartless. We are coming for them. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Declare the landslide a natural terracing project and announce future parks.
options.advisor_reason: Rebranding a tragedy as a 50M park project is incredibly bleak. But if we just call it green space, maybe people will eventually forget the homes underneath.

consequences.follow_up: The sheer audacity leaves the public speechless. The PR team resigns en masse.
consequences.city_reaction: They called our destroyed homes a park. Unbelievable. {city.slang_reaction}.
